title: All SSH key logins fail after an sshd config change
description_md:
Nobody can log in with their SSH key anymore — every key login falls through to a password prompt, for every user. A 'security hardening' change to the sshd config went wrong. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), find the setting that disabled key auth, re-enable it, and reload sshd.

A test key is at `~/deploy_key` (`ssh -i ~/deploy_key deploy@localhost`). You're done when that key login succeeds.
